message.content:
Repo: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-1). Venv: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python.
/tmp/pass1_reconfirm.json is a JSON array of 10 pass-1 findings that were strengthened after a first confirmation pass found them 'appropriate but incomplete'. Each item has: issue, title, file, problem, fix_update (what was just added/changed).
Constraints (NOT bugs): local-first/data-sovereign, no metered API, engine imports no adapter, lazy optional backends.

You are independent CONFIRMER #1 of 2 for INDEX 4 (0-based) in /tmp/pass1_reconfirm.json.
Read that item (issue, problem, fix_update). Then verify the CURRENT state on branch harden/pass-1: read the changed source AND the named regression test(s), and run a PoC / the specific tests against the venv where useful. Decide: appropriate = the fix soundly addresses the root cause (no hack/over-claim); complete = it fully resolves the finding AND has a regression test that would actually catch a regression (a test that passes equally with/without the fix does NOT count). If the item documents a deliberately-deferred scope (e.g. #119's Protocol-boundary note), judge completeness against that honestly-scoped fix, not an unbounded ideal. Set either false with specifics if not met. Echo the issue number.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content: 1	[{"issue": "102", "title": "run_json applies retries multiplicatively, causing a retry-storm and unbounded blocking time with no overall deadline", "file": "src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py", "sev": "HIGH", "problem": "`run_json` loops `MAX_RETRIES+1` times (line 531) and each iteration calls `_run_with_retries` (line 542), which itself retries up to `MAX_RETRIES` transient attempts AND up to `RATE_LIMIT_MAX_RETRIES` rate-limit attempts. The budgets multiply. PoC (transient errors every attempt): `_attempt` is invoked 9 times = (MAX_RETRIES+1)^2, not the intended ~3. Worse, the rate-limit path: per outer iteration the rate budget sleeps sum(RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF*(r+1) for r in 0..4) = 450s; times 3 outer iteratio", "fix_update": "run_json now re-rolls ONLY on JSON parse failure (transient/rate/permanent propagate, no budget multiplication) + _sleep_bounded bounds inner backoff by the overall deadline. New tests: no-multiplication, seed re-roll on parse failure, deadline bound."}, {"issue": "111", "title": "Graph-orchestration gate decisions never enter the hash-chained provenance trail \u2014 the documented \u201cappend-only trail\u201d for the durable path is unchained, untamper-evident LangGraph state", "file": "/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/orchestration/gatenode.py", "sev": "MEDIUM", "problem": "There are two governance mechanisms (ADR-0010): the synchronous run_study path records every gate decision into the hash-chained ProvenanceTrail (provenance.py), and the durable LangGraph interrupt/resume path (gatenode.make_gate_node) records decisions as plain dicts appended to a LangGraph state list 'gate_decisions'. GOVERNANCE \u00a73 states the chained ProvenanceTrail \"is the spine\u2026 No step can be retroactively removed or reordered,\" and GOVERNANCE.md lines 47-50 call the graph's gate_decisions ", "fix_update": "make_gate_node already accepts recorder= (bridges to ProvenanceTrail); now GOVERNANCE \u00a73 + graph.py docstring honestly scope that the durable graph path needs the recorder to be hash-chained. Test test_gate_node_bridges_decision_into_provenance_trail."}, {"issue": "113", "title": "GateReview is a frozen dataclass whose Mapping payload is not frozen \u2014 mutable-in-place and unhashable (issue #7 escape)", "file": "src/assay_engine/pipeline.py", "sev": "LOW", "problem": "GateReview is declared @dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True) with a `payload: Mapping[str, Any]` field but, unlike EVERY other frozen mapping-bearing value record in the engine (ProvenanceEntry, GateDecision, Corpus, ClaimRecord, Verdict, Hypothesis, BaselineArtifact, ReasoningRequest, etc.), it has no __post_init__ that normalizes the field through freeze_mapping. This reproduces both halves of the codebase's own documented issue #7 (see src/assay_engine/_frozen.py module docstring):\n\n1. Mutable-", "fix_update": "GateReview.__post_init__ freezes payload; NEW test test_gate_review_payload_is_frozen_and_hashable asserts FrozenDict + mutation raises + hashable."}, {"issue": "114", "title": "ProvenanceTrail.record performs a non-atomic check-then-act on shared list state (not thread-safe)", "file": "src/assay_engine/provenance.py", "sev": "LOW", "problem": "record() computes `seq = len(self._entries)` and `prev_hash = self._entries[-1].entry_hash` (read), then does work (clock, digest, freeze), then `self._entries.append(entry)` (write), with no lock. If a single ProvenanceTrail were shared across threads, two concurrent record() calls could read the same seq/prev_hash and append two entries that both claim e.g. seq=N with the same prev_hash, breaking the chain so verify_records() (which asserts e.seq == i and e.prev_hash == prev) would fail \u2014 a co", "fix_update": "record() lock-guarded; NEW test test_record_is_thread_safe_under_concurrency (8 threads x50, verify + contiguous seqs)."}, {"issue": "117", "title": "QdrantVectorStore owns and never closes its self-constructed client (connection/handle leak)", "file": "src/assay_engine/persistence/vectorstore.py", "sev": "LOW", "problem": "When no client is injected, `QdrantVectorStore.__init__` constructs its own `QdrantClient` via `get_qdrant_client()` (line 64) and stores it on `self._client`, but the class exposes no `close()`/context-manager and never closes the client. `QdrantClient` holds an underlying HTTP connection pool (httpx) / gRPC channel; for a self-constructed client the store is the sole owner, so the connection resources are leaked for the lifetime of every store instance with no deterministic release. Long-runni", "fix_update": "_owns_client + close()/context-manager; NEW test test_vector_store_closes_owned_client (owned client closed via get_qdrant_client + context exit)."}, {"issue": "118", "title": "QdrantVectorStore.upsert materializes all PointStructs (with per-vector copies) before sending any batch, so batching bounds payload but not memory", "file": "src/assay_engine/persistence/vectorstore.py", "sev": "LOW", "problem": "upsert builds the complete `points` list \u2014 one PointStruct per id, each holding `list(v)` (a fresh copy of the whole vector) \u2014 for the entire input before the chunking loop at line 88 sends anything. The comment claims chunking handles 'a large ingest', but it only bounds each HTTP request payload; peak process memory still holds the full corpus of vectors duplicated into PointStructs at once. For a real ingest (e.g. 5M docs x 768 float dims) this is the entire embedding set re-copied into Pytho", "fix_update": "upsert builds one batch at a time; NEW test test_vector_store_upsert_builds_one_batch_at_a_time asserts only batch_size vectors consumed per send (2,4,6 not 6,6,6)."}, {"issue": "119", "title": "corpus_fingerprint is recomputed redundantly per study (ingest + every build_baseline_artifact call), each a full canonical copy + sort + JSON of the whole corpus", "file": "src/assay_engine/baseline/determinism.py", "sev": "LOW", "problem": "corpus_fingerprint builds a full list-comprehension copy of every unit and relation, sorts all units by id, canonicalizes every attribute mapping via _plain, and json.dumps the whole thing \u2014 O(total corpus bytes) time and memory. run_study computes it once at ingest (pipeline.py:280, `cfp`). Then build_baseline_artifact (determinism.py:104) computes it again from scratch for the determinism record's `input_hashes['corpus']` and `corpus_fingerprint`; a study's BaselineBuilder that calls build_bas", "fix_update": "build_baseline_artifact accepts corpus_hash (no recompute); NEW test test_build_baseline_reuses_precomputed_corpus_hash. NOTE: full e2e dedup across the BaselineBuilder Protocol boundary is deliberately deferred (would require a breaking build() signature change for a LOW perf item) \u2014 documented scope."}, {"issue": "120", "title": "Package version is hardcoded in two independent places (pyproject `version` and `__init__.__version__`), and the second feeds the reproducibility/provenance manifest", "file": "pyproject.toml", "sev": "LOW", "problem": "`pyproject.toml` sets `version = \"0.0.1\"` (line 7) while `src/assay_engine/__init__.py` separately hardcodes `__version__ = \"0.0.1\"` (line 28). These are two independent strings with no single source of truth \u2014 hatchling is not configured for dynamic versioning (`grep dynamic`/`hatch.version` returns nothing). This is not merely a style nit: `src/assay_engine/baseline/determinism.py` imports `__version__ as _ENGINE_VERSION` (line 24) and stamps it into the determinism/provenance record as `compo", "fix_update": "version dynamic + hatch.version path; NEW tests in test_packaging.py (no static version, dynamic declared, dist==__version__)."}, {"issue": "121", "title": "mypy is pinned to `python_version = \"3.11\"` but CI advertises and matrix-tests up to 3.14, so 3.12\u20133.14-only type errors are never caught", "file": "pyproject.toml", "sev": "LOW", "problem": "`[tool.mypy] python_version = \"3.11\"` forces mypy to type-check the code as if running on Python 3.11 regardless of the runner's interpreter. The core CI lane runs mypy across a 3.11\u20133.14 matrix and the integration lane runs it on 3.12, but because the analysis target is fixed at 3.11, type behavior that differs across versions (e.g. stdlib stubs that gained/changed signatures in 3.12+, version-gated typing constructs) is only ever validated at the 3.11 floor. The project classifies and matrix-t", "fix_update": "mypy python_version removed; NEW test test_mypy_is_not_pinned_to_a_single_python_version parses pyproject."}, {"issue": "122", "title": "run_study emits phase/reasoning spans but never starts tracing or stamps run_id baggage \u2014 traces cannot be correlated to the run or the experiment-tracker run_id", "file": "/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/pipeline.py", "sev": "LOW", "problem": "run_study is the documented end-to-end composed runner and it opens trace spans for every phase (tracer.span('phase:INGEST') etc.) and the reasoning seam emits LLM/AGENT spans underneath. But run_study never calls bootstrap_tracing() and never wraps execution in run_trace_context(run_id) \u2014 grep of pipeline.py shows no reference to either. run_trace_context (tracing.py:145-163) is what stamps the 'assay.run_id' baggage onto every span so traces are correlatable across stores; the orchestration gr", "fix_update": "run_study calls bootstrap_tracing + wraps phases in run_trace_context(corr_id); NEW test test_run_study_correlates_spans_to_run_id asserts every phase span sees assay.run_id baggage + detached after."}]

content.text: Index 4 is issue #117 (QdrantVectorStore client leak). Let me read the source and tests.

content.content:
1	"""Local vector store seam (ADR-0003) — loopback-enforced Qdrant factory.
2	
3	Ported and generalized from the prior platform. The client is constructed only against a
4	loopback host; the guard fires *before* importing the (optional ``persistence`` extra)
5	``qdrant_client``, so a misconfigured remote host is rejected regardless of whether the
6	package is installed.
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	import os
12	import uuid
13	from typing import Any, Protocol, Sequence, runtime_checkable
14	
15	from assay_engine._local import require_loopback_host
16	
17	VECTOR_HOST = os.environ.get("ASSAY_VECTOR_HOST", "localhost")
18	VECTOR_HTTP_PORT = int(os.environ.get("ASSAY_VECTOR_HTTP_PORT", "6333"))
19	
20	# Stable namespace for mapping the engine's string ids onto Qdrant point UUIDs.
21	_POINT_NS = uuid.UUID("a55a9e00-0000-4000-8000-000000000001")
22	
23	
24	@runtime_checkable
25	class VectorStore(Protocol):
26	    def upsert(self, ids: Sequence[str], vectors: Sequence[Sequence[float]]) -> None: ...
27	    def query(self, vector: Sequence[float], k: int) -> list[tuple[str, float]]: ...
28	
29	
30	def vector_store_url() -> str:
31	    host = require_loopback_host(VECTOR_HOST, what="vector store host")
32	    return f"http://{host}:{VECTOR_HTTP_PORT}"
33	
34	
35	def get_qdrant_client() -> Any:
36	    """Construct a loopback-only Qdrant client (lazy import of the optional extra)."""
37	    host = require_loopback_host(VECTOR_HOST, what="vector store host")  # before any import
38	    try:
39	        from qdrant_client import QdrantClient
40	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
41	        raise RuntimeError(
42	            "vector store requires the 'persistence' extra (qdrant-client) — not installed"
43	        ) from exc
44	    return QdrantClient(host=host, port=VECTOR_HTTP_PORT)
45	
46	
47	def _point_id(ref: str) -> str:
48	    """Deterministic Qdrant point UUID for an engine string id (Qdrant ids must be int/UUID)."""
49	    return str(uuid.uuid5(_POINT_NS, ref))
50	
51	
52	class QdrantVectorStore:
53	    """Reference :class:`VectorStore` over a loopback Qdrant collection (cosine).
54	
55	    The engine's ids are arbitrary strings; Qdrant point ids must be int/UUID, so each id is
56	    mapped to a deterministic UUID and the original id is stored in the point payload (and
57	    returned by ``query``). This is a goal-agnostic reference implementation; a study may
58	    supply its own ``VectorStore`` (collection naming, distance, sharding are study choices).
59	    """
60	
61	    def __init__(self, collection: str, dim: int, *, client: Any | None = None) -> None:
62	        self._collection = collection
63	        self._dim = dim
64	        # Track ownership: only close a client we created ourselves; an injected client is the
65	        # caller's to manage (#117).
66	        self._owns_client = client is None
67	        self._client = client if client is not None else get_qdrant_client()
68	
69	    def close(self) -> None:
70	        """Close the underlying client if this store created it (no-op for an injected one)."""
71	        if self._owns_client:
72	            close = getattr(self._client, "close", None)
73	            if callable(close):
74	                close()
75	
76	    def __enter__(self) -> "QdrantVectorStore":
77	        return self
78	
79	    def __exit__(self, *_exc: object) -> None:
80	        self.close()
81	
82	    def ensure_collection(self) -> None:
83	        from qdrant_client.models import Distance, VectorParams
84	
85	        if not self._client.collection_exists(self._collection):
86	            self._client.create_collection(
87	                self._collection,
88	                vectors_config=VectorParams(size=self._dim, distance=Distance.COSINE),
89	            )
90	
91	    def upsert(
92	        self, ids: Sequence[str], vectors: Sequence[Sequence[float]], *, batch_size: int = 256
93	    ) -> None:
94	        from qdrant_client.models import PointStruct
95	
96	        if len(ids) != len(vectors):
97	            raise ValueError("ids and vectors must have equal length")
98	        if batch_size < 1:
99	            raise ValueError("batch_size must be >= 1")
100	        # Build each batch of PointStructs lazily, so only one batch is materialized in memory at
101	        # a time (bounding BOTH payload AND memory) rather than boxing the whole corpus up front
102	        # (#118). Qdrant guidance: don't upload point-by-point, but bound the batch.
103	        for start in range(0, len(ids), batch_size):
104	            batch = [
105	                PointStruct(id=_point_id(ids[j]), vector=list(vectors[j]), payload={"ref": ids[j]})
106	                for j in range(start, min(start + batch_size, len(ids)))
107	            ]
108	            self._client.upsert(self._collection, points=batch)
109	
110	    def query(self, vector: Sequence[float], k: int) -> list[tuple[str, float]]:
111	        res = self._client.query_points(
112	            self._collection, query=list(vector), limit=k, with_payload=True
113	        )
114	        return [(p.payload["ref"], float(p.score)) for p in res.points]
115

tests/test_persistence.py:315:def test_vector_store_upsert_builds_one_batch_at_a_time():

230	    with pytest.raises(NonLocalEndpointError):
231	        vector_store_url()

234	
235	
236	# ---- content-addressed versioning ----
237	
238	
239	def test_versioner_is_deterministic_and_addressable(tmp_path):
240	    store = tmp_path / "store"
241	    a = tmp_path / "a.txt"
242	    a.write_text("hello world")
243	    v = LocalDataVersioner(store_dir=str(store))
244	    digest1 = v.put(str(a))
245	    digest2 = v.fingerprint(str(a))
246	    assert digest1 == digest2  # put and fingerprint agree
247	    assert v.path_for(digest1).is_file()  # artifact is retrievable by id
248	    # identical bytes elsewhere -> identical id (content-addressed)
249	    b = tmp_path / "b.txt"
250	    b.write_text("hello world")
251	    assert v.put(str(b)) == digest1
252	
253	
254	def test_versioner_rejects_non_file(tmp_path):
255	    v = LocalDataVersioner(store_dir=str(tmp_path / "s"))
256	    with pytest.raises(FileNotFoundError):
257	        v.fingerprint(str(tmp_path / "missing"))
258	
259	
260	def test_versioner_path_for_rejects_traversal(tmp_path):
261	    # #112: path_for must reject non-digest ids that would escape the store
262	    v = LocalDataVersioner(store_dir=str(tmp_path / "s"))
263	    for bad in ["../../etc/passwd", "..", "a/b", "g" * 64, "abc", "/etc/passwd"]:
264	        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="invalid version id"):
265	            v.path_for(bad)
266	    assert v.path_for("a" * 64).name == "a" * 64  # a valid digest is accepted
267	
268	
269	def test_vector_store_upsert_streams_batches_and_owns_client():
270	    import pytest as _pytest
271	
272	    _pytest.importorskip("qdrant_client")
273	    from assay_engine.persistence.vectorstore import QdrantVectorStore
274	
275	    class FakeClient:
276	        def __init__(self):
277	            self.batches = []
278	            self.closed = False
279	
280	        def upsert(self, collection, points):
281	            self.batches.append(len(points))
282	
283	        def close(self):
284	            self.closed = True
285	
286	    fc = FakeClient()
287	    store = QdrantVectorStore("c", 2, client=fc)
288	    store.upsert([f"u{i}" for i in range(5)], [[0.0, 1.0]] * 5, batch_size=2)
289	    assert fc.batches == [2, 2, 1]  # #118: streamed in bounded batches
290	    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
291	        store.upsert(["u0"], [[0.0], [1.0]])  # length mismatch
292	    store.close()
293	    assert fc.closed is False  # #117: an INJECTED client is the caller's to close, not ours
294	
295	
296	def test_vector_store_closes_owned_client(monkeypatch):
297	    # #117: a self-created client (no client injected) MUST be closed on close()/context-exit
298	    pytest.importorskip("qdrant_client")
299	    from assay_engine.persistence import vectorstore as vs
300	
301	    class FakeClient:
302	        def __init__(self):
303	            self.closed = False
304	
305	        def close(self):
306	            self.closed = True
307	
308	    created = FakeClient()
309	    monkeypatch.setattr(vs, "get_qdrant_client", lambda: created)
310	    with vs.QdrantVectorStore("c", 2) as store:  # no client injected → owns it
311	        assert store._owns_client is True
312	    assert created.closed is True  # owned client closed on context exit (leak prevented)
313	
314	
315	def test_vector_store_upsert_builds_one_batch_at_a_time():
316	    # #118: only one batch of PointStructs may be materialized at a time — assert construction is
317	    # interleaved with sends, not all-up-front.
318	    pytest.importorskip("qdrant_client")
319	    from assay_engine.persistence.vectorstore import QdrantVectorStore
320	
321	    accessed: list[int] = []
322	
323	    class TrackingVectors:
324	        def __init__(self, data):
325	            self._d = data
326	
327	        def __len__(self):
328	            return len(self._d)
329	
330	        def __getitem__(self, i):
331	            accessed.append(i)  # records when each vector is consumed
332	            return self._d[i]
333	
334	    class FakeClient:
335	        def __init__(self):
336	            self.accessed_at_call = []
337	
338	        def upsert(self, collection, points):
339	            self.accessed_at_call.append(len(accessed))  # how many vectors consumed so far
340	
341	    fc = FakeClient()
342	    store = QdrantVectorStore("c", 2, client=fc)
343	    store.upsert([f"u{i}" for i in range(6)], TrackingVectors([[0.0, 1.0]] * 6), batch_size=2)
344	    # per-batch construction → 2,4,6 consumed at each send; all-up-front would be 6,6,6
345	    assert fc.accessed_at_call == [2, 4, 6]
346	
347	
348	# ---- hardened checkpointer invariants (fake backends injected, no Postgres) ----
349

content.text: The test is well-targeted for #117. Let me verify qdrant_client is installed in the venv and run the relevant test.

input.command: cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && .venv/bin/python -c "import qdrant_client; print('qdrant installed')" 2>&1; .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_persistence.py::test_vector_store_closes_owned_client tests/test_persistence.py::test_vector_store_upsert_streams_batches_and_owns_client -q 2>&1 | tail -25

content.text:
Now let me verify the test would actually catch a regression by checking that it fails without the fix. The key assertion is `created.closed is True`. Without `close()` closing an owned client, that assertion would fail. Let me confirm by examining the test logic against a counterfactual.

The test injects no client (so `_owns_client` is True), monkeypatches `get_qdrant_client` to return a tracking fake, enters/exits the context manager, and asserts the owned client was closed. Without the `close()` implementation (the fix), `created.closed` would remain False and the assertion fails. So this test genuinely catches a regression.

Let me also verify the counterpart assertion (injected client NOT closed) is meaningful by confirming the `_owns_client` gating.

The fix correctly:
- Sets `_owns_client = client is None` (line 66)
- `close()` only closes when owned (lines 71-74), using a safe `getattr`/`callable` guard
- Provides context-manager protocol (`__enter__`/`__exit__`)

Both halves are tested: owned client IS closed (test_vector_store_closes_owned_client), injected client is NOT closed (test_vector_store_upsert_streams_batches_and_owns_client line 293). This soundly addresses the root cause (resource leak for self-constructed clients) without breaking the injected-client ownership contract.

input.reasoning:
Issue #117: QdrantVectorStore owned and never closed a self-constructed QdrantClient (httpx pool / gRPC channel leak). The current state on harden/pass-1 soundly addresses the root cause: __init__ records ownership via _owns_client = client is None (line 66); a new close() (lines 69-74) closes the underlying client ONLY when owned, guarded by getattr/callable so it is safe even if the client lacks close(); and __enter__/__exit__ (lines 76-80) add context-manager support so the resource is released deterministically. This is the correct, non-hacky fix and it does not over-claim — it deliberately leaves an injected client to the caller, which is the right ownership contract.

Complete: both halves are covered by targeted regression tests. test_vector_store_closes_owned_client (lines 296-312) injects NO client (so the store owns it), monkeypatches get_qdrant_client to a fake, uses the store as a context manager, and asserts created.closed is True — this would FAIL without the close()/__exit__ fix (the fake would never be closed), so it genuinely catches a regression. test_vector_store_upsert_streams_batches_and_owns_client asserts the complementary invariant (injected client fc.closed is False after store.close(), line 293), which would FAIL if close() ignored ownership and closed an injected client. The fix correctly resolves the named LOW finding with regression coverage that actually discriminates fixed vs. unfixed behavior.
input.evidence: Source: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/persistence/vectorstore.py lines 61-80 (_owns_client, close(), __enter__/__exit__). Tests: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/tests/test_persistence.py lines 269-293 (injected client NOT closed) and 296-312 (owned client IS closed on context exit). Ran in venv: qdrant_client installed; `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_persistence.py::test_vector_store_closes_owned_client tests/test_persistence.py::test_vector_store_upsert_streams_batches_and_owns_client -q` => 2 passed. Counterfactual: removing close()/__exit__ would leave created.closed False, failing line 312 — test discriminates fix from no-fix.
